title: Bulkhead Isolation
description:
Apply bulkhead limits so best-effort workloads cannot starve critical workloads.

### Objective
Implement the FastAPI service so the replayed evaluator requests pass exactly as specified.

### Implement These Routes
- `POST /integrations/bulkhead`

### Request Replay (Executed In Order)
1. `POST /integrations/bulkhead`
   Request body: `{"critical":[1],"best_effort":[1,2,3],"best_effort_limit":2}`
   Expected status: `200`
   Expected body: `{"critical_processed":1,"best_effort_processed":2}`

2. `POST /integrations/bulkhead`
   Request body: `{"critical":[1,2],"best_effort":[],"best_effort_limit":1}`
   Expected status: `200`
   Expected body: `{"critical_processed":2,"best_effort_processed":0}`

3. `POST /integrations/bulkhead`
   Request body: `{"critical":[],"best_effort":[1,2],"best_effort_limit":5}`
   Expected status: `200`
   Expected body: `{"critical_processed":0,"best_effort_processed":2}`

### Candidate Expectations
- Complete the TODOs in starter code and keep behavior deterministic across sequential requests.
- Keep state in memory for this challenge; do not use SQL or external databases.
- Return exact status codes and JSON response shapes expected by the replay tests.
- Handle invalid or missing input defensively with stable JSON errors instead of crashes.

### Starter Files You Can Edit
- `app/main.py`
- `app/schemas.py`
- `app/service.py`

### Done Criteria
- All replayed request tests pass in order.
- API behavior matches the required contracts exactly.
constraints:
- Python 3.11+
- No SQL or external database usage
- Deterministic outputs for graded requests
